stimulus: To bring her work to more readers, poet Carmen Lozano turned to community broadsides. In broadside printing, a poem is printed on a single large sheet, often illustrated and distributed in cafés, libraries, and meeting halls. In her broadside series Mothers of the Valley (1972–1974), Lozano paired short poems about the daily lives of farmworking mothers in the Central Valley with brief tributes to lesser-known organizers from the same region. The pairing invites the reader to see ties among the women. The broadside format helped Lozano's poetry reach a wide audience and supported her aim of building solidarity among Latina women through her work.
stem: According to the text, what is significant about Lozano's use of broadside printing?

answerOptions.content: Broadside printing relied on materials that were inexpensive and easy for Lozano to obtain.
Lozano became financially successful once she began using broadside printing.
Broadside printing helped Lozano use poetry to bring people together, especially Latina women.
Lozano was one of the first Latina poets to use broadside printing.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The text states that the broadside format helped Lozano's poetry reach a wide audience and supported her aim of building solidarity among Latina women.

Choice A is incorrect; the text does not discuss the cost or availability of materials. Choice B is incorrect; the text mentions a wide audience but does not say Lozano became financially successful. Choice D is incorrect; the text does not claim Lozano was among the first Latina poets to use broadsides.
